text.title: tmux Power User Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the tmux Power User methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Prefix MUST be C-a (or C-Space) — never the default C-b. C-b conflicts with shell backward-word on operator's daily keybindings.
rule.rationale: Default prefix collides with bash/zsh `backward-word`; remapping prevents constant accidental tmux command-mode.

rule.statement: Plugins MUST be declared in tmux.conf with version pins, installed via TPM (~/.tmux/plugins/tpm). No copy-paste from blog posts.
rule.rationale: TPM gives reproducible updates; floating plugin versions break unexpectedly.

rule.statement: set-option -g history-limit 100000. Lower values lose context on long-running sessions.
rule.rationale: Default 2000 is laughably small for debug sessions; 100k uses negligible memory.

rule.statement: TmuxPlan names a single owner. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
Copy-mode bindings MUST detect OS: macOS uses `pbcopy`, Linux uses `xclip` or `wl-copy`. Single hardcoded command breaks on the other OS.
rule.rationale: Cross-platform operators paste the same tmux.conf to both; conditional logic prevents 'why doesn't copy work' moments.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-tmux-power-user.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
